title: Don’t Miss the Cycle: Four Considerations for Capital Investment Decisions
summary: After several years of supply chain disruptions, tariff uncertainty, and volatile demand patterns, skepticism toward economic momentum is understandable.

title: Manufacturing Momentum: Economic Trends Driving the Industry
summary: After several years of uneven recovery, the US manufacturing sector is beginning to show renewed momentum.

title: Real Personal Income: A Trend We Are Watching
summary: Growth in US Real Personal Income (excluding current transfer receipts), a measure of inflation-adjusted income that excludes transfer payments like workers compensation or unemployment benefits, slowed to 1.2% (annual trend) for January 2026.

title: Preparing for the 2030s: Key Questions Leaders Are Asking
summary: We received many thoughtful questions regarding our economic outlook for the next decade during our December 2025 Executive Series webinar.

title: Oil Prices and the Economy: Forecast, Consumer Risk, and Inflation Impact
summary: Clients have been asking a lot of questions about oil prices and their economic impact lately. Where are prices headed? How will consumers handle the shock of higher energy costs? Is there a price point at which oil prices begin to adversely impact the economy? How should I plan for inflation? Here is what we want you to know.

title: The Quiet Operational Advantage
summary: At ITR Economics, we stress that preparation — not reaction — rewards businesses.When growth is mild and margins tighten, strong leaders need to focus on the fundamentals that can quietly erode profitability when ignored.

title: What We’re Seeing in Consumer Trends
summary: Consumer spending accounts for roughly two-thirds of US GDP. As the consumer goes, so goes the broader economy.

title: Growth Can Return While Profitability Lags
summary: That is the reality many leaders face when costs remain elevated and pricing power is limited. ITR Economics has been warning that the 2020s may include periods of profitless prosperity — where top-line growth is achievable, but margins are compressed by labor, materials, interest rates, and other structural pressures.

title: Business Investment a Constructive Signal Despite Uncertainty
summary: Recent data show a notable rise in business investment, suggesting that firms are still committing resources toward productive capacity. US Capital Goods New Orders are in in an accelerating growth trend, coming in at 14.9% above the year-ago level in November 2025.

Press Highlights
Building the law of abundance
summary:
An emerging movement known as “the Abundance Agenda” or “the State Capacity Movement” is drawing attention to how lackluster government performance underwrites a host of problems in the United States, ranging from crumbling infrastructure to inadequate housing to rampant tax evasion.
The post Building the law of abundance appeared first on Niskanen Center.

title: Niskanen Center, Arnold Ventures applaud Utah clearance rate legislation
summary:
The Niskanen Center and Arnold Ventures applaud the Utah State Legislature and Governor Spencer Cox for passing new, bipartisan legislation designed to solve more crimes and provide support for crime victims.
The post Niskanen Center, Arnold Ventures applaud Utah clearance rate legislation appeared first on Niskanen Center.

title: Reforming our way to dynamism, with Philip K. Howard
summary:
Howard offers qualified approval of the Abundance movement that he in some ways anticipated by decades.
The post Reforming our way to dynamism, with Philip K. Howard appeared first on Niskanen Center.

title: The future of innovation, with Andrew McAfee
summary:
Brink Lindsey welcomes Andrew McAfee to discuss the current state and future prospects of technological and economic dynamism. 
The post The future of innovation, with Andrew McAfee appeared first on Niskanen Center.

title: We can’t ‘incarcerate our way out of crime.’ But we can deter a lot more of it.
summary:
Musk’s claim that incarceration works to control crime is the beginning of the analysis, not the end. The next question is whether it works better than the available alternatives. 
The post We can’t ‘incarcerate our way out of crime.’ But we can deter a lot more of it. appeared first on Niskanen Center.

title: Next-generation geothermal power: A commercial readiness assessment
summary:
The potential amount of power that could be generated by a mature geothermal industry, and the geographic flexibility of the technology, could significantly alter the generation mix across the United States.
The post Next-generation geothermal power: A commercial readiness assessment appeared first on Niskanen Center.

title: Evaluation as due process: Civil Service in an automated age
summary:
Modern government is built on the civil service. The twentieth century consensus, emerging out of the New Deal, was based on a social safety net administered by civil servants to ensure fair administration and due process.
The post Evaluation as due process: Civil Service in an automated age appeared first on Niskanen Center.

title: Op-Ed: Transportation policy is incomplete without housing
summary:
American families shouldn’t pay twice for federal siloing — once in unaffordable housing, and again in long commutes. 
The post Op-Ed: Transportation policy is incomplete without housing appeared first on Niskanen Center.

title: A capacity agenda for state departments of education
summary:
The 2024 National Assessment of Educational Progress (NAEP) revealed a sobering reality for most of the nation: public school students had not achieved the post-pandemic recovery that policymakers and educators hoped for. While fourth-grade mathematics scores rose slightly, reading scores declined to their lowest level in decades. Data from NWEA’s MAP assessment in 2025 tells […]
The post A capacity agenda for state departments of education appeared first on Niskanen Center.

title: Testimony: State of the bulk power system
summary:
Testimony of Dr. Liza Reed, Director of Climate and Energy Policy at Niskanen Center for the United States Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee Hearing: “State of the Bulk Power System.”

Military Career Paths: Do Adolescents See a Future in the Military?
summary: In this piece, we examine key trends in how often Ukrainian adolescents consider service in the Armed Forces of Ukraine and aspire to a military career.

title: Does a Pension Deficit Really Exist in Ukraine? What Share of Pension Fund Expenditures Is Covered by the State Budget?
summary: The more people who pay a portion of their wages as pension contributions (the Unified Social Contribution, USC) to the Pension Fund, the more funds are available for pension payments. Where does Ukraine stand in this regard?

title: More Reforms Don’t Always Mean More Progress – A 2025 Reform Review
summary: Which changes are truly moving the country forward, and which are raising concerns among experts – we explore these questions in this annual review of reforms.

title: Ukraine’s Digital Transformation: What Could Accelerate or Stall the Reform
summary: How to sustain the pace and intensity of digital transformation amid war, terror against Ukraine’s energy infrastructure, leadership changes in state institutions, and rising security risks.

title: The Usual Year-End Surge Did Not Materialize: Overview of Key Changes in Q4 2025
summary: The last quarter of 2025 brought a total of 42 reforms and one zero-scoring change in the socio-economic sphere to the country’s reform record.

title: Important Draft Laws, Issue 63: Tax Incentives for Businesses in Kyiv, Tax Exemption for Individuals Renting Out Housing
summary: Between February 2 and 15, a total of 27 draft laws were registered in the Verkhovna Rada: 2 presidential, 11 government-initiated, and 14 introduced by MPs.

title: TikTok Landing of Muraiev: How A Politician from the Past Is Returning to the Ukrainian Information Space
summary: VoxCheck explains how a network of bots and “empty” accounts is trying to revive Muraiev, and what messages he is promoting in 2026.

title: Tourism Challenges in the Carpathians During the Full-Scale Invasion
summary: During the full-scale Russian invasion, domestic tourism has grown significantly.

summary: Can intermunicipal cooperation (IMC) strengthen community resilience during wartime?

title: The Light Returns–But What About Trust? Will the Warmth Between Us Fade?
summary: The loss of electricity and heat was meant to exhaust and break us—but did it actually succeed?

title: The human cost of unsafe abortions
summary: Romania’s history offers a rare natural experiment on what happens when abortion laws change rapidly. What can the rest of the world learn from this?

title: Why cheap waste management is key to stopping plastic pollution
summary: Improving waste management in low- and middle-income countries could cut global pollution by 98%.

title: What are the world’s deadliest animals, and can we protect ourselves against them?
summary: Deaths from other animals are mostly caused by just two types: mosquitoes and snakes.

title: Tracking historical progress against slavery and forced labor: a long-run data view
summary: Almost all countries have ended large-scale forced labor, often surprisingly recently.

title: Four minutes of air conditioning
summary: Billions of people have access to far less electricity per day than is required to run an air conditioner for just one hour.
